things.name: Kastel Fortress
things.description: Ancient Roman and later Ottoman fortress on the Vrbas riverbank; beautifully restored with ramparts, towers and a riverside park popular with locals

things.name: Christ the Saviour Cathedral
things.description: The largest Serbian Orthodox cathedral in the Western Balkans, consecrated in 2004 after the 1990s war, with a soaring tower and rich interior mosaics

things.name: Ferhadija Mosque
things.description: Rebuilt Ottoman mosque from the 16th century; the original was destroyed in the 1993 war and the painstaking reconstruction was completed in 2016

things.name: Vrbas River Rafting
things.description: The emerald-green Vrbas canyon offers excellent white-water rafting through dramatic gorges just minutes from the city centre

things.name: Museum of the Republika Srpska
things.description: Regional museum with natural history, archaeology and art collections covering the history of this Bosnian Serb entity and the wider Balkans region

things.name: Gospodska Street
things.description: Main pedestrian promenade at the heart of the city, lined with outdoor cafes, shops and AustroHungarian architecture; the social pulse of Banja Luka

things.name: Republic Square
things.description: The central square of Banja Luka with the Government of Republika Srpska building and the large open plaza hosting concerts and civic events

things.name: Zoo Banja Luka
things.description: Compact city zoo in the Mlaka area with a collection of animals from wolves and bears to exotic species, popular with families at weekends

things.name: Krupa na Vrbasu Monastery
things.description: Medieval Serbian Orthodox monastery set in a dramatic river gorge below Banja Luka, surrounded by lush forest and cascading waterfalls

things.name: Gradski Stadion Banja Luka
things.description: Home of FK Borac Banja Luka, the city's leading club and 2024-25 UEFA Conference League group stage participants
